content.problemStatement:
In Olympics, the countries are ranked by the **total number of medals won**. You are given six integers G1G_1G1​, S1S_1S1​, B1B_1B1​, and G2G_2G2​, S2S_2S2​, B2B_2B2​, the number of gold, silver and bronze medals won by two different countries respectively. Determine which country is ranked better on the leaderboard.  It is guaranteed that there will not be a tie between the two countries.


Input Format


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.




The first and only line of each test case contains six space-separated integers G1G_1G1​, S1S_1S1​, B1B_1B1​, and G2G_2G2​, S2S_2S2​, B2B_2B2​.





Output Format
For each test case, print `"1"` if the first country is ranked better or `"2"` otherwise. Output the answer without quotes.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the total medals of the two countries are never equal.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains six space‑separated integers G1 S1 B1 G2 S2 B2 – the gold, silver and bronze medals of the first and second country respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "1" if the first country has a larger total number of medals, otherwise output "2".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: sums are 60 vs 59 → output 1. Test case 2: sums are 0 vs 1 → output 2. Test case 3: sums are 3 vs 0 → output 1.
First country has 0 medals, second has 1 → second ranks better, so output 2.
First country total = 900, second total = 897 → first ranks better, output 1.

solution.approach: For each test case compute sum1 = G1+S1+B1 and sum2 = G2+S2+B2. Because a tie cannot occur, simply compare the two sums: if sum1>sum2 output 1 else output 2. This is O(1) work per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        g1=int(next(it)); s1=int(next(it)); b1=int(next(it))
        g2=int(next(it)); s2=int(next(it)); b2=int(next(it))
        sum1=g1+s1+b1
        sum2=g2+s2+b2
        out.append('1' if sum1>sum2 else '2')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    solve()
